items.summary: Legendary investor Robert Kirby stresses that beating the market demands perspective, patience, and courage. These qualities are crucial for navigating market cycles and volatility. Long-term wealth creation depends on emotional discipline and consistency. Investors must focus on the journey, not just opportunities.

The partnership emphasizes speed, convenience, and reliability for digitally savvy young travelers.

items.title: Fredun Pharmaceuticals board approves 2:1 bonus issue
items.summary: Fredun Pharmaceuticals’ board has recommended a 2:1 bonus issue, granting two new equity shares for every one held. This decision aims to reward shareholders, reflecting management's confidence in the company’s sustained earnings growth, scalability, and long-term value creation strategy.

items.title: Brigade Group inks pact for Rs 850 cr residential development in Hyderabad
items.summary: Brigade Group has signed a Joint Development Agreement for a premium residential project in Hyderabad's Kompally area. The project has a revenue potential of Rs 850 crores. This expansion highlights Hyderabad's importance as a growth market for Brigade. The company plans to invest Rs 5,000 crores in various developments over the next 3-4 years.

items.summary: Coal India has directed subsidiaries to ramp up coal supplies to power plants as an intense heatwave pushed India’s electricity demand to a record high, raising concerns over shortages and power cuts.

items.summary: Retail inflation may reach 5 percent by June due to fuel price increases and higher gold and silver import duties. Economists believe the Reserve Bank of India will observe the impact of these hikes before considering interest rate changes in the latter half of the fiscal year.

items.title: Sebi chief Tuhin Kanta Pandey backs bond ETFs, tokenisation as debt fundraising nears Rs 9 lakh crore
items.summary: Sebi chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey called for deeper development of India’s corporate bond market to support long-term economic growth. He highlighted rising debt fundraising, proposed bond ETFs, stronger disclosures and tokenisation pilots, while urging greater retail participation and reduced dependence on bank-led financing.

items.title: WFH wherever feasible, restrict foreign travel, Maruti Suzuki tells employees
items.summary: Maruti Suzuki India is adopting austerity measures. The company is promoting work from home where possible. Foreign travel is being restricted unless essential for business. Employees are encouraged to use virtual meetings and minimize domestic travel. Sustainable commute options like carpooling and public transport are also promoted. Energy conservation is a key focus across offices and homes.

items.summary: Maruti Suzuki India Ltd is adopting austerity and efficiency measures. These steps aim to conserve fuel and reduce foreign exchange expenditure. The company is encouraging work from home where possible. Travel is being minimized, with virtual meetings preferred. Employees are urged to carpool and use public transport. Offices will optimize energy use.

items.summary: Pakistan is proposing a bonded storage plan to boost its energy security by allowing international suppliers to hold petroleum stocks. The government aims to build strategic reserves, funded by a levy on fuel, and increase local supply capacity. This initiative comes as Pakistan lacks strategic petroleum reserves despite heavy reliance on Gulf imports.

items.summary: ​Domestic markets ended lower on Monday. LIC and Campus Activewear gained on strong triggers, while Pine Labs and RVNL declined after quarterly earnings.

items.summary: Zee Entertainment Enterprises has appointed Shipra Wahi as Chief Sales Officer for its streaming platform, Zee5. Wealth managers are also partnering for integrated real estate advice.

items.title: Is Quad the Asian NATO or ocean foam? What Delhi meet reveals
items.summary: Quad foreign ministers met in Delhi focusing on practical cooperation. New initiatives target critical minerals, maritime surveillance, and energy security. The grouping is shifting towards economic and technological coordination. This pragmatic approach aims for resilience rather than deterrence. The Quad's future hinges on US strategic consistency and its ability to adapt.

items.title: Q-Line Biotech IPO allotment to be finalised today. Here's GMP, steps to check status

items.title: India cancels soymeal export deals, turns to Africa for soybean imports
items.summary: Indian traders have cancelled 25,000 metric tons of soymeal export contracts. This is the first time since 2021. They have also booked 80,000 tons of imports from African countries. Soaring domestic prices have reversed trade flows. This shift helps North and South American suppliers boost shipments to Asian buyers. African exporters are selling oilseeds at a premium.

items.title: Threading Europe: India’s textile majors redraw strategy after tariff turbulence
items.summary: Indian textile and apparel companies are expanding their presence in Europe and the UK. Upcoming free trade agreements are expected to boost India's competitiveness against rival sourcing hubs. Companies are setting up showrooms and increasing participation in trade fairs. This diversification aims to reduce dependence on single export markets.

items.title: Bernstein says Aadhar Housing, Home First, other housing stocks can rally up to 34%. Here are 3 reasons why
items.summary: Bernstein sees a significant rally potential of up to 34% in affordable housing finance stocks like Home First Finance and Aptus Value Housing. Favorable trends, improving business momentum, and stable operating conditions are driving this optimism. Despite attractive valuations, the sector benefits from low mortgage penetration and a strong competitive moat.

items.title: Smallcap stock Exhicon Events jumps despite tepid trends in the Indian stock market

items.title: Astra Microwave Products shares rise over 12% to fresh record high after Q4 results

items.title: AAIB to publish an interim report on Air India crash by June

items.title: Fuel price hikes narrow oil companies’ losses to ₹600 crore a day
items.summary: With Monday’s hike of ₹2.61 per litre, petrol prices crossed the ₹100-per-litre mark in the national capital to retail at ₹102.12 per litre in Delhi, according to data from Indian Oil Corporation.

items.title: OMC stocks close sharply higher as Brent settles near $95 and fourth fuel hike shores up margins
items.summary: HPCL led gains with a 5.8% rise to ₹412.55 on the NSE, BPCL advanced over 4% and IOC was up 3.7%

items.title: Nifty crosses 24,000 on US-Iran peace hopes, crude plunge
items.summary: The Nifty 50 closed at 24,031.70, up 312.40 points (1.32%), marking its first close above 24,000 in recent sessions; The BSE Sensex ended at 76,488.96, gaining 1,073.61 points (1.42%)

items.title: India emerges as Sri Lanka's second-largest export destination
items.summary: India is now Sri Lanka's second-largest export market, overtaking the United Kingdom. This shift occurred between January and April of this year. The United States remains the top destination for Sri Lankan exports. Sri Lanka's overall exports saw growth in April, driven by merchandise and services. Coconut products and IT services performed well.

items.title: Indian entrepreneur explains how Warren Buffett shaped his financial habits: ‘I don't have single rupee of black money’
items.summary: The entrepreneur credited Buffett as one of the biggest influences in his life and stressed the importance of choosing the right role models.

items.title: SC rules exxsol hexane a pure hydrocarbon, duty cut to 2.5%
items.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that exxsol hexane is a pure hydrocarbon. This decision means imported exxsol hexane will attract a lower customs duty of 2.5 percent. Previously, customs officials wanted it classified as motor spirit, which carried a 10 percent duty. The court upheld a tribunal's order favoring the lower duty. This ruling impacts the nation's import-export status.

items.title: Government forwent Rs 1 lakh crore via excise duty cuts to shield consumers from fuel inflation: FM
items.summary: India's Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman explains recent fuel price increases are market-driven by oil companies. She noted the government previously absorbed over Rs 1 lakh crore in revenue through excise duty cuts to protect consumers from inflation. The minister also discussed managing global challenges and the evolving landscape for textile exporters, emphasizing sustainability and technology adoption.

items.title: FM Sitharaman says Government open to hear investor concerns on LTCG, STCG taxation
items.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has indicated the government is ready to listen to stock market investors. Concerns regarding Long-Term Capital Gains and Short-Term Capital Gains taxes are being considered. The Minister emphasized openness to feedback on taxation matters. This comes amid market volatility and discussions on investor sentiment. The government is engaging with stakeholders on market-related tax issues.

items.title: India may end refined copper import dependence this year, says Hindalco MD
items.summary: The push for self-reliance in copper aligns with India’s broader industrial strategy, particularly as demand accelerates from energy transition sectors.

items.title: Two LNG tankers pass through Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Another vessel carrying crude oil crossed the vital shipping lane on Saturday

items.title: IRFC expands beyond Indian Railways with ₹13,527-crore refinancing deal for Hyderabad Metro
items.summary: The state-backed financier is leveraging its core strength in transportation finance to support urban mobility and help the Telangana government-owned project replace its high-cost debt.

items.title: India proposes broad revamp of industrial output index ahead of new series
items.summary: India's statistics ministry is set to unveil a new way to measure industrial output next week. Key changes include expanding the sectors tracked and adopting a chain-linked framework. The base year will be updated, and new sectors like renewable power and waste management will be included. This aims to provide a more accurate picture of India's evolving industrial landscape.

items.title: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal
items.summary: Trump said on Saturday that an agreement would include the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, without giving further details.

items.title: European stocks highest since March 2 as U.S.-Iran talks continue; euro zone bond yields drop on peace hopes
items.summary: Stocks in Europe are tracking their Asian counterparts higher after Japan's Nikkei 225 breached 65,000 for the first time.

items.title: FM asks lenders to go beyond standard loans, design credit repayments around biz cycles
items.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has called on banks to move beyond standard loan products. She emphasized the need for repayment structures that align with the specific income cycles of different businesses. This initiative aims to provide tailored credit solutions for MSMEs, supporting their growth and contributing to a stronger middle class and a developed India.

items.title: Infra projects see cost overrun of Rs 5.65 lakh crore in April
items.summary: Central sector infrastructure projects exceeding Rs 150 crore each show a cumulative cost overrun of Rs 5.65 lakh crore. As of April, 1,981 projects are monitored with a revised cost of Rs 42.78 lakh crore. Expenditure stands at Rs 20.36 lakh crore, indicating steady implementation. Transport and logistics lead with the highest number of projects and revised costs.

items.title: RBI to examine role of quantum technology in strengthening financial sector
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is forming an expert committee to study quantum technology's role in finance. This new technology could revolutionize financial tasks but also poses security risks. The committee will assess current systems and recommend ways to make India's financial sector quantum-safe. The report is expected within six months.

items.title: India's wheat procurement surges past 34 million tonnes, bolsters food buffers
items.summary: Punjab led the procurement process, with around 12.16 mt of wheat purchased by government agencies, followed by Madhya Pradesh with 10.14 mt and Haryana 8.12 mt. Among the other wheat-producing states, Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh purchased around 2.1 mt and 1.55 mt respectively until 24 May.

items.title: The 90% vulnerability: Inside Ajoy Chawla’s plan to grow Titan beyond jewellery
items.summary: A stellar quarter and ₹76,000 crore in annual revenue couldn’t save Titan from a brutal market crash. As gold tariffs rise and macro shocks hit Tanishq, can the retail giant successfully pivot to a diversified lifestyle future?

items.title: When are prediction markets most helpful? Evercore ISI has a formula
items.summary: Evercore ISI strategists provide insight for when prediction markets are useful for forecasting.

items.title: Adequate availability of fertilisers for upcoming Kharif sowing season: Govt
items.summary: India is set for a robust Kharif season with ample fertilizer supplies. Domestic production and imports have ensured comfortable stock levels. Over 200 lakh tonne of fertilizers are currently available, exceeding 50 percent of the projected demand. This proactive measure guarantees sufficient soil nutrient availability for farmers as sowing begins in June.

items.title: Beware the boom and bust cycle of memory stocks, investors warn amid AI excitement
items.summary: "In the long run, it's a pretty dreadful industry," William de Gale of BlueBox Asset Management, told CNBC's Europe Early Edition on Wednesday.

items.title: PSU closure: Govt sets in motion new move after years wasted in stop-start process
items.summary: The Centre is pushing ministries to accelerate the closure of unprofitable state-owned companies. Progress has been slow despite a policy approved eight years ago. Only one company has effectively ceased operations. The government aims to expedite these closures, addressing concerns over delays in both disinvestment and shutdown processes.

items.title: West Asia conflict may dent India Inc profitability by 200 bps: Crisil
items.summary: A prolonged West Asia conflict poses a risk to India Inc's profitability. Crisil Ratings predicts a significant drop in operating margins due to supply chain issues and rising costs. However, strong company balance sheets are expected to protect overall credit quality. Some sectors like ceramics and airlines face severe stress. Other export-oriented sectors might benefit from a weaker rupee.

items.title: Schroders Capital plans to build on $1 bn India investment as scope for early investor exits grows beyond IPOs
items.summary: The company is scouting for opportunities in sectors including consumer, healthcare, technology and artificial intelligence.

items.title: India’s AI boom could turn data centres into the next big infrastructure race: KPMG
items.summary: India's data centre sector is set for massive growth, reaching nearly $46 billion by 2033. AI workloads and cloud adoption are key drivers. The market is shifting towards AI-optimised infrastructure. Challenges lie in execution complexity, with a call for integrated lifecycle partners. Data localisation laws and ESG priorities are also boosting demand for local data centres.

items.title: Singapore reports lower-than-expected inflation for April at 1.8%, revises economic growth higher
items.summary: Core inflation — which strips out prices of private transport and accommodation — came in at 1.4%, against estimates of 1.7%.

items.title: FM Nirmala Sitharaman reveals petrol & diesel excise duty cut will hit govt revenues by ₹1 lakh crore
items.summary: Nirmala Sitharaman has confirmed that cutting excise duties on petrol and diesel will result in a revenue loss of ₹1 lakh crore. She also pushed back against a 'pessimistic narrative' on India's economy

items.summary: Chinese telecom giant Huawei announced Monday a new way of designing chips that improves their capabilities, despite U.S. restrictions on the company.

items.summary: Roseate Hotels is shifting to an asset-light strategy and expanding into pilgrimage and lifestyle businesses as competition intensifies in India’s luxury hospitality sector.

items.title: Who was Toshifumi Suzuki? All about the founder of Japan’s iconic Seven‑Eleven stores who died at 93
items.summary: Toshifumi Suzuki, the former chairman of Seven & i Holdings and founder of Seven-Eleven Japan, has died at 93. Credited with transforming Japan’s retail industry, Suzuki introduced the convenience store franchise model in 1973 and helped build Seven-Eleven into a global retail giant.

items.title: Huawei plans 1.4nm chips by 2031 as China races to catch TSMC
items.summary: Huawei is working to reduce its five-year semiconductor production gap with TSMC by producing 1.4-nanometer chips by 2031.

items.summary: Brent crude fell over 4% below $100 a barrel on hopes of an Iran-US peace deal, but Indian fuel retailers raised petrol and diesel prices for the fourth time in two weeks.

items.summary: Shopsy is being rebuilt around feeds, rewards and entertainment as Flipkart prioritizes digital habit formation over immediate purchases in India’s hyper-value e-commerce market.

items.title: India's solar export boom collapses as US tariffs bite
items.summary: All but one of India’s top listed solar module companies reported nil exports during the quarter ended 31 March. Market leader Waaree Energies Ltd, which had so far managed to protect its exports to the US, also reported fewer outbound shipments during the fourth quarter of FY26.

items.title: India's EV cell makers see first real shot at closing China price gap
items.summary: Lithium-ion cell makers are intensifying talks with the government to cut costs and build a local supplier base, as a 9% rise in imported Chinese cell prices offers a window to narrow the price gap. The push is urgent as EV cell imports hit $4.6 billion in FY26, over two-thirds of it from China.

items.title: Visa-backed PayMate ties unpaid employee dues to long-pending DigiAsia deal, funding
items.summary: PayMate, the Visa-backed B2B payments firm that once chased a ₹1,500-crore IPO, has been unable to pay full salaries to some staff for up to a year, linking repayment to the closure of a restructured deal with DigiAsia Corp.

items.title: AI promised cost savings, but Microsoft and Uber say it’s costing more than human workers
items.summary: Microsoft has cancelled most of its Claude Code licences and Uber burned through its 2026 AI budget in four months. New data reveals why enterprise AI costs are rising even as token prices fall.

items.summary: H-1B visa approvals for India’s top IT services firms fell 40% this year to 11,041. As the Trump administration cracks down on work visas and green cards, IT firms are shifting towards more offshore work and local hiring in the US.
